name: Kount Fraud Detection and Identity API
what_it_does: Kount fraud detection and identity trust REST API for e-commerce merchants and payment processors to assess transaction risk, detect fraud, and make real-time approve/decline decisions using behavioral analytics, device fingerprinting, and machine learning models backed by Equifax's identity network for chargeback prevention and account takeover protection. Enables AI agents to manage real-time fraud inquiry for transaction risk assessment automation, handle device fingerprinting for digital identity trust automation, access order review and decision for fraud workflow automation, retrieve identity trust global network signals for risk enrichment automation, manage chargeback dispute data for fraud analytics automation, handle account takeover detection for login fraud automation, access rules-based custom policies for fraud threshold automation, retrieve risk signal enrichment for payment authorization automation, manage agent console integration for manual review workflow automation, and integrate Kount with Magento, WooCommerce, Salesforce Commerce, and enterprise payment platforms for end-to-end fraud prevention automation.
use_cases: Assessing e-commerce transaction fraud risk from payment fraud agents
Detecting account takeover attempts from identity protection agents
Automating chargeback-risk order review from fraud workflow agents
Enriching payment authorization with fraud signals from risk agents
not_for: SMB simple fraud rules (Kount is enterprise ML-powered, not simple rules)
Identity verification/KYC (use Persona or Onfido for document identity verification)
AML/compliance screening (use ComplyAdvantage for AML/sanctions screening)
best_when: An e-commerce merchant or payment processor wanting AI agents to automate real-time fraud risk assessment, device fingerprinting, and account takeover detection through Kount's Equifax-backed behavioral analytics platform.
avoid_when: CLIENT-SIDE SDK REQUIRED FOR DEVICE FINGERPRINTING: Kount device fingerprinting requires client-side JavaScript (Data Collector) to collect device signals; automated server-side risk assessment without device fingerprint has significantly reduced fraud signal quality; automated fraud assessment without device fingerprint creates lower accuracy risk scoring. MODEL TRAINING REQUIRES DATA VOLUME: Kount ML models improve with merchant-specific transaction history; automated fraud detection for new merchants without Kount history creates generic model performance; automated deployment without training data warming creates suboptimal initial fraud detection. INQUIRY AND UPDATE SEQUENCE: Kount uses inquiry (pre-auth risk check) and update (post-auth outcome); automated fraud workflow must call both inquiry and update in sequence; automated inquiry-only without update creates Kount unable to learn from transaction outcomes. EQUIFAX NETWORK SUBSCRIPTION: Kount Identity Trust Global Network data requires Equifax data subscription; automated maximum risk signal requires appropriate Equifax data access tier; automated entry-tier access creates reduced identity network enrichment.

auth.notes: Kount uses Merchant ID + API Key for authentication. REST API with JSON/XML. Boise, ID HQ. Founded 2007 by Brad Wiskirchen. Acquired by Equifax for $640M in 2021. Products: Fraud inquiry, device fingerprint, identity trust, account takeover protection, chargeback management. SDKs: Java, .NET, PHP, Python, Node.js. PCI DSS. SOC2. GDPR. Serves enterprise e-commerce. Competes with Riskified and Signifyd for e-commerce fraud.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise sales process required
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-inquiry pricing; typically $0.01-0.10 per transaction assessed; volume tiers
pricing.notes: Boise ID. Equifax subsidiary. Per-inquiry pricing. Enterprise annual contract typical.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per merchant; inquiry APIs high-throughput for checkout; webhooks for review decisions

known_agent_gotchas: CLIENT-SIDE DATA COLLECTOR REQUIRED: Kount fraud accuracy requires client-side JavaScript Data Collector to send device fingerprint (KHASH); automated server-side API without client Data Collector creates severely degraded fraud signal; automated device fingerprint must be collected client-side before server-side inquiry

INQUIRY-UPDATE LIFECYCLE: Kount requires inquiry (pre-auth check) followed by update (post-auth result); automated fraud workflow must close loop with update; automated inquiry-only creates Kount cannot learn from outcomes and degrades model accuracy over time

KOUNT SCORE 0-100 REQUIRES MERCHANT THRESHOLD: Kount returns 0-100 risk score; automated decline threshold must be configured per merchant risk tolerance; automated fixed 50-threshold assumption creates wrong risk calibration for merchant's fraud/approval tradeoff
XML vs JSON RESPONSE MODE: Kount supports both XML and JSON responses; automated integration must specify response mode; automated JSON parsing of XML response creates parse failure

AUTO_RESPONSE DECISION vs MANUAL REVIEW: Kount returns AUTO (automatic decision) and REVIEW (needs human review) orders; automated fulfillment must handle REVIEW state; automated AUTO-only fulfillment skips review-flagged orders creating potential fraud acceptance
agent_readiness.security_notes: Fraud prevention. PCI-DSS, SOC2, GDPR. API key. US/EU. Transaction and device fingerprint data. Equifax subsidiary.

security_score_components.security_notes: Fraud prevention. PCI-DSS, SOC2. API key. US. Transaction risk and identity data.
